title: Community & tax base
intro: Who and what the Town of Riverhead taxes. Population sets the scale of demand for services; the tax base — the assessed value of all taxable property — is what the levy is spread across. A few large commercial properties carry an outsized share, which is why grievance settlements on them ripple onto everyone else's bill.

population.source: U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts, Riverhead town, Suffolk County, NY.

taxBase.impliedFullValuationNote: Implied from the audited debt-limit disclosure: the statutory debt limit is 7% of the Town's five-year-average full (market) valuation, and that limit is $495,782,621 — so the five-year-average full valuation is about $7.08 billion.
taxBase.assessmentRatioNote: Riverhead assesses property at roughly 8% of market value — an assessed value of about $50,040 corresponds to a ~$600,000 market-value home (near the Town median).
taxBase.townWideRateChange2023: The town-wide tax rate rose $2.409 per $1,000 of assessed value in 2023.
taxBase.assessedChange2023: Total assessed value barely moved in 2023 — up $380,869, or 0.05%, from 2022.
taxBase.transferTax: Since a 1998 voter referendum, Riverhead levies a 2% real-estate transfer tax (the Community Preservation Fund) on sales above $150,000 (developed) / $75,000 (undeveloped).

largestTaxpayers.note: The Town does not publish a ranked principal-taxpayers schedule in its basic financial statements, so this is a list of the major known commercial ratables from public reporting rather than an official assessed-value ranking. Tanger is repeatedly described as the Town's single largest tax generator.

items.name: Tanger Outlets (Tanger Factory Outlet Centers)
items.note: Two outlet centers on Route 58 — repeatedly cited as the Town's largest single tax generator.

items.name: PSEG Long Island / utility infrastructure
items.note: Electric transmission and distribution property is a major utility ratable.

items.name: Costco Wholesale
items.note: Big-box anchor; has litigated its assessment (a claimed ~$20.3M over-valuation).

items.name: Walmart
items.note: Big-box anchor; won a settlement cutting its assessed value ~$950,000 over five years.

items.name: Route 58 big-box retail corridor
items.note: Home Depot, Lowe's, Target and neighbors form a concentrated commercial tax base.

items.name: EPCAL / Calverton Enterprise Park
items.note: The former Grumman site — a large publicly-influenced parcel the Town has worked for years to return to the tax rolls.

assessmentStress.headline: Riverhead's assessment system is under chronic strain.
points: More than 300 tax-grievance lawsuits are filed against the Town every year, and settlements shift the burden onto other taxpayers.
A decades-old assessment error tied to the Friar's Head golf property produced tax spikes of up to ~160% for some residents in 2026.
Because assessed values sit at ~8% of market and are not regularly reassessed town-wide, small percentage swings move large dollar amounts.

sources: U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts — Riverhead town, Suffolk County, NY.
Town of Riverhead 2022 Audited Basic Financial Statements (debt-limit and assessed-value disclosures).
RiverheadLOCAL and Riverhead News-Review reporting on Tanger, big-box assessments, grievance litigation, and the Friar's Head refund (2022, 2024, 2026).
